advice:
Take Trey McBride right now. Here's why:

TE is the single biggest positional cliff on this board. McBride (VORP +70.85) and Brock Bowers (VORP +67.25) are the only two TEs with elite separation from replacement level — and both are sitting right here at pick 12 with ADPs of 20.7 and 20.6. You have 5 picks until your next turn, and three of the four gap teams have already taken a WR. None have taken a TE yet, but with both top TEs on the board and 5 picks to burn, the odds that at least one — and possibly both — are gone before you pick again are very real.

The survivability risk of waiting is severe. TE replacement level is 123.40 points. McBride's projection is 194.25 — a +70.85 VORP gap. The next tier of TEs will likely be 30-40 VORP points lower. That's a season-long disadvantage you can't recover from at a position where you'll be starting someone every week.

Your Zero-RB strategy is working — you already have James Cook. WR is a need, but Amon-Ra St. Brown (VORP +89.84) and Justin Jefferson (VORP +61.64) have shallower cliffs below them; quality WR options like Nico Collins, A.J. Brown, and George Pickens are still available in the next few rounds. There is no comparable TE fallback.

McBride also has a late bye (Week 14), no injury flag, and is the unquestioned TE1 in Arizona. Grab him now — the risk of waiting even one round is too high.
